title: Review: diversified_address + payment address wiring
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — The address reuse bug (nie-cs0.1) is low probability in practice but breaks the privacy invariant when it occurs; set_diversifier_index monotonicity gap (nie-cs0.3) is a latent time bomb. The API surface issues (nie-cs0.4, nie-cs0.5) will create maintenance cost when the next feature lands. The passphrase UX (nie-cs0.6) will generate user reports.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) I cannot verify the Sapling diversifier validity distribution for the zero-seed at the indices tested — if the test seed produces runs of valid diversifiers at low indices, nie-cs0.1 would be impossible to trigger in tests; (2) I have not traced the ZIP-316 UA encoding path through zcash_address under different network types to confirm 'utest1' is the correct bech32m prefix for testnet (it looks right but I am not citing a spec). Would raise confidence if: an external ZIP-316 test vector at a non-zero diversifier index were added (closes nie-cs0.2), and if the SQLite error injection path for nie-cs0.1 were explicitly tested.
